definition: Stable event identifier.
Dataset accession or benchmark label.
Species or biological system.
Pathway, module, lineage, perturbation, or multiome event family.
Best single event mode when identifiable.
Uncertainty-aware set of plausible event modes, when available.
Signed or absolute event strength depending on module.
Direction such as up, down, delay, target-down-alt-up, or artifact.
Estimated event onset in real time or pseudotime.
Estimated peak event time.
Event-level FDR or comparable adjusted p-value.
Sample, embryo, donor, or block-level robustness measure.
Matched or counterfactual effect after state adjustment.
maintext_status: Required when composition artifact is plausible

definition: Design-level recognizability score.
Margin over random, irrelevant, ribosome, stress, or shuffled controls.
Boolean gates used by ClaimBoundary.
Strongest allowed evidence tier / claim boundary.
Plain-language biological claim allowed.
Claim explicitly blocked by missing gates.
Evidence needed for next evidence tier.
RNA, ATAC, multiome, lineage, perturbation, metadata scaffold.
Source file used to produce this object.
